column1: 012023020100,"Here are some sentences in Apurinã and their approximate English equivalents:

column2: I gave my canoe to her.
I gave my canoe to her.
The man brought my blood to the woman.
She brought your_{sg} banana to the man.
The man gave the woman’s bone to me.
He brought the man’s eye to me.
She found the girl.","Translate into English:

column1: 19. kyky aikute pysykary ywamukary","['She remembered the woman.', 'I ate the banana.', 'You_{sg} gave the man’s house to him.']",eng_Latn,['apu_Latn'],translation,single

column1: 012023030100,"Here are some sentences in Coastal Marind and their English translations:
